title: fire_threatening_residential
description: A rapidly advancing wildfire moves into a densely populated residential neighborhood with limited egress routes.

timeline.title: Wildfire Reaches Residential Zone
timeline.description: Fire crosses into the first residential street. Initiate immediate evacuation.

timeline.title: Multiple Homes Ignited
timeline.description: Three homes now burning. Focus on containment and ensuring evacuation routes are clear.

timeline.title: Traffic Gridlock on Exit Roads
timeline.description: Residents fleeing clog the only two exit roads. Coordinate traffic control.

timeline.title: Stranded Residents Reported
timeline.description: Multiple households unable to evacuate due to gridlock. Arrange rescue transport.

timeline.title: Fire Line Established
timeline.description: Crews establish a defensive line at the community park to slow further spread.

timeline.title: Neighborhood Cleared
timeline.description: All residents accounted for. Fire contained to initial impact zone.

node-1.title: Fire Enters Neighborhood
node-1.situation: A fast-moving wildfire has jumped a firebreak and entered the edge of a residential neighborhood of 1,200 homes. Winds are driving flames down multiple streets. There are two exit roads.
node-1.question: What is your immediate priority?

options.label: A
options.text: Issue a mandatory evacuation order immediately and dispatch law enforcement to manage traffic on exit roads.

options.label: B
options.text: Prioritize fire suppression on the entry point to prevent further spread into the neighborhood.

options.label: C
options.text: Wait for additional fire crews before issuing evacuation to avoid premature panic.

node-2.title: Evacuation Traffic Crisis
node-2.situation: Both exit roads are gridlocked with evacuating vehicles. Residents are abandoning cars and blocking the roadway. The fire is 3 streets away and advancing.
node-2.question: How do you relieve the traffic gridlock?

options.label: A
options.text: Assign officers to direct traffic, open a fire lane as a one-way exit, and clear abandoned vehicles.

options.label: B
options.text: Reverse the gridlock by closing one exit road and funneling all traffic to the other.

node-3.title: Stranded Residents
node-3.situation: Dispatch receives reports of elderly and mobility-impaired residents unable to evacuate on foot or by vehicle. Their addresses span multiple streets now surrounded by smoke.
node-3.question: What rescue strategy do you deploy?

options.label: A
options.text: Dispatch buses and rescue vehicles with police escorts to extract stranded residents by address priority.

options.label: B
options.text: Instruct stranded residents to shelter in place and wait for fire to pass.

node-4.title: Defensive Line Decision
node-4.situation: Fire crews recommend establishing a defensive line at the community park, which has open grass and a parking lot that could serve as a buffer. This would sacrifice several blocks of homes already burning.
node-4.question: Do you approve the defensive line at the park?

options.label: A
options.text: Approve the park defensive line to protect the majority of the neighborhood and focus resources there.

options.label: B
options.text: Reject the line and continue direct attack on all burning homes to save as many as possible.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: All residents have been evacuated or rescued. The defensive line held and protected 80% of the neighborhood. Accountability checks confirm no civilian casualties.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
